(née **Čelnova**; born 21 September 1976) is a retired Latvian [long-distance](/wiki/Long-distance_track_event "Long-distance track event") [runner](/wiki/Running "Running"), best known for winning the [New York City Marathon](/wiki/New_York_City_Marathon "New York City Marathon") in 2005 and 2006.

Evans, Hilary; Gjerde, Arild; Heijmans, Jeroen; [Mallon, Bill](/wiki/Bill_Mallon "Bill Mallon"); et al. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Latvian long-distance runner

Jeļena Prokopčuka

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Prokopčuka at the [2015 New York City Marathon](/wiki/2015_New_York_City_Marathon "2015 New York City Marathon") | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (1976-09-21) 21 September 1976 (age 49)  [Riga](/wiki/Riga "Riga"), [Latvian SSR](/wiki/Latvian_SSR "Latvian SSR"), Soviet Union |
| Height | 168 cm (5 ft 6 in) |
| Weight | 52 kg (115 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | Athletics |

**Jeļena Prokopčuka** (née **Čelnova**; born 21 September 1976) is a retired Latvian [long-distance](/wiki/Long-distance_track_event "Long-distance track event") [runner](/wiki/Running "Running"), best known for winning the [New York City Marathon](/wiki/New_York_City_Marathon "New York City Marathon") in 2005 and 2006.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Je%C4%BCena_Prokop%C4%8Duka&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

She holds six outdoor and one indoor [Latvian record](/wiki/Latvian_records_in_athletics "Latvian records in athletics"), ranging from [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres") to the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") distance. Her husband, [Aleksandrs Prokopčuks](/wiki/Aleksandrs_Prokop%C4%8Duks "Aleksandrs Prokopčuks"), holds the men's Latvian marathon record with 2:15:56 hours. She is a three-time participant at the [Summer Olympics](/wiki/Summer_Olympics "Summer Olympics"), having represented Latvia in track events in 1996, 2000 and 2004.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Prokopčuka won the 2001 [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon"). She won the [Paris Half Marathon](/wiki/Paris_Half_Marathon "Paris Half Marathon") in 2002, 2003[[2]](#cite_note-2) and 2009.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She was the 2003 champion at the [World Military Cross Country Championships](/wiki/World_Military_Cross_Country_Championships "World Military Cross Country Championships"). She won the [Great Edinburgh Run](/wiki/Great_Edinburgh_Run "Great Edinburgh Run") three times consecutively from 2005 to 2007. She is also a five-time winner of the [Kuldīga Half Marathon](/wiki/Kuld%C4%ABga_Half_Marathon "Kuldīga Half Marathon"), and a two-time winner of the [Almond Blossom Cross Country](/wiki/Almond_Blossom_Cross_Country "Almond Blossom Cross Country"). Elsewhere, she won the 2002 [20 Kilomètres de Paris](/wiki/20_Kilom%C3%A8tres_de_Paris "20 Kilomètres de Paris"), the [Osaka International Ladies Marathon](/wiki/Osaka_International_Ladies_Marathon "Osaka International Ladies Marathon") in 2005 (where she ran her national record time of 2:22:56 hours), and the [San Silvestre Vallecana](/wiki/San_Silvestre_Vallecana "San Silvestre Vallecana") in 2006.

She was the top-performing European at the 2012 [Lisbon Half Marathon](/wiki/Lisbon_Half_Marathon "Lisbon Half Marathon"), coming fifth overall.[[4]](#cite_note-4) She was tenth at the [2012 London Marathon](/wiki/2012_London_Marathon "2012 London Marathon") and won the [Riga Half Marathon](/wiki/Riga_Half_Marathon "Riga Half Marathon"). She surprised at the [Great North Run](/wiki/Great_North_Run "Great North Run"), leading out the elite women, and although she fell to fourth she ran a national record of 68:09 minutes.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Her final outing of the year came at the [Yokohama Marathon](/wiki/Yokohama_Marathon "Yokohama Marathon"), where her season's best time of 2:26:55 hours brought her fourth place.

A strong finish at the 2013 [Nagoya Marathon](/wiki/Nagoya_Marathon "Nagoya Marathon") saw her climb from 16th to fourth and record a time of 2:25:46 hours – her fastest in six years.[[6]](#cite_note-6) She was runner-up to [Tirunesh Dibaba](/wiki/Tirunesh_Dibaba "Tirunesh Dibaba") at the [Great Manchester Run](/wiki/Great_Manchester_Run "Great Manchester Run") in May.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
